message:
fix(runtime_compat): route select! through __select_private re-exports, not ::futures

Follow-up hardening on the `#[macro_export]`-ed `select!` macro so it
works for downstream callers that do not have `futures` listed as a
direct dependency. Previously the macro body referred to
`::futures::pin_mut!`, `::futures::future::select`,
`::futures::future::Either` — absolute paths that require the crate
`futures` to be resolvable at the call site. Any crate that depended
only on `frankenterm_core` and used `frankenterm_core::runtime_compat::
select!{...}` would fail to expand with "could not find crate
`futures` in the list of loaded crates".

Fix: add a `#[doc(hidden)] pub mod __select_private { pub use
futures::future::{Either, select}; pub use futures::pin_mut; }` inside
`runtime_compat` and switch every `::futures::...` reference inside
the macro body to `$crate::runtime_compat::__select_private::...`.
Downstream crates now only need `frankenterm_core` reachable at the
call site; `$crate` resolves to this crate unconditionally and the
hidden re-export module provides every symbol the macro needs.

Applies to all six arms (`@poll2`, `@poll3`, and each of the public
two-branch/three-branch shapes). No new behavior; the expansion is
equivalent but uses the re-exports.
